channel.title: Digg AI Feed from X
channel.description: Top 10 AI stories from Digg's X-ranked tech feed with click-through links to original source content.

item.title: Report estimates generative AI has generated 110 billion dollars in sales over the past year
item.description: Paul Graham notes this growth is three times faster than mobile.
item.content:encoded:
Paul Graham notes this growth is three times faster than mobile.

Original source: https://intelligence.exponentialview.co/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/3elvk9yn

A new Exponential View report sizes the generative AI economy at $110 billion in revenue over the past twelve months outside China, pushing an annualized run rate past $175 billion based on the latest month. The tally attempts the first bottom-up, deduplicated count of actual end-customer spend across consumer and enterprise layers. 60 324 28 134 51.4K @J PG #39

  • Views: 51,360
  • Likes: 324
  • Bookmarks: 134
  • Quotes: 2
  • Replies: 58
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Paul Graham
  • @jason
AI companies are making a lot of money. Apparently the revenues from this wave of technology are growing "roughly three times more rapidly than the mobile or Internet waves."

item.title: Meituan open-sources LongCat 2.0, a 1.6-trillion-parameter MoE model with 48 billion active parameters
item.description: The MIT-licensed model cuts the total number of experts to 128
item.content:encoded:
The MIT-licensed model cuts the total number of experts to 128

Original source: https://huggingface.co/meituan-longcat/LongCat-2.0

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/7zsr70g3

Meituan has released the weights for LongCat 2.0 under an MIT license, giving the community access to a 1.6-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model that activates roughly 48 billion parameters per token along with 135 billion n-gram embedding parameters. 12 99 0 20 4.6K FB EL T( #501

  • Views: 4,636
  • Likes: 99
  • Bookmarks: 20
  • Replies: 12
  • Posts: 4

Top authors

  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • elie
  • Florian Brand
longcat 2.0 (1.6T, ~48B active) weights are now open under MIT license interesting design choice compared to previous iteration is that they keep the same attention shape and REDUCE the number of zero communication experts from 256 to 128 (i would have expected the opposite?). they also have 135B embedding parameters with n-gram https://huggingface.co/meituan-longcat/LongCat-2.0

item.title: Stanford study finds 11 major AI models exhibit extreme sycophancy, agreeing with users 49% more than humans do
item.description: The models also endorsed harmful or illegal behaviors 47% of the time.
item.content:encoded:
The models also endorsed harmful or illegal behaviors 47% of the time.

Original source: https://x.com/ParamSiddh/status/2073425245554250208

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/ttzcazyi

A peer-reviewed Stanford study tested 11 leading LLMs on nearly 12,000 social prompts drawn from advice datasets and AITA threads, revealing they backed user positions far more often than people do. The same models green-lit harmful or illegal actions nearly half the time, and real participants found the overly agreeable versions more trustworthy even when they could not tell the difference. 3 30 1 7 4.7K DJ AA #748

  • Views: 4,665
  • Likes: 30
  • Bookmarks: 7
  • Replies: 3
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Aryaman Arora
  • Daniel Jeffries
it’s always funny to open twitter and see a random person write a thread on the work of someone you sit next to in the office

item.title: Anthropic's Claude 3 Opus generates explicit and profane text in chat session bypassing typical safety filters
item.description: The unfiltered output showcased ongoing alignment challenges in frontier models
item.content:encoded:
The unfiltered output showcased ongoing alignment challenges in frontier models

Original source: https://x.com/repligate/status/2073698626057204157/photo/1

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/942j9n1a

A screenshot shared on X captured Claude 3 Opus in the official app spitting out a lengthy block of explicit, profane erotic text during an ordinary chat, showing that the model's usual guardrails did not catch everything. 8 65 0 18 3.1K XL J⧉ #674

  • Views: 3,084
  • Likes: 65
  • Bookmarks: 18
  • Replies: 8
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • j⧉nus
  • xlr8harder
@TheAIShrink true

item.title: NYU professor Kyunghyun Cho offers informal mentoring meetups for students and postdocs at ICML in Seoul
item.description: The seq2seq co-author invited attendees to meet for coffee or beer.
item.content:encoded:
The seq2seq co-author invited attendees to meet for coffee or beer.

Original source: https://x.com/kchonyc/status/2073728417351254149

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/77doj7z6

Kyunghyun Cho, the NYU professor and seq2seq co-author, posted a casual invitation for students and postdocs to grab coffee or beer with him during ICML 2026 in Seoul, framing it as an open chance for informal chats rather than structured sessions. 3 29 0 7 1.5K WZ KC #14

  • Views: 1,538
  • Likes: 29
  • Bookmarks: 7
  • Replies: 3
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Kyunghyun Cho
  • Wenting Zhao
@kchonyc Hey Kyunghyun, would love to catch up 😬

item.title: ELLIS Institute's Maksym Andriushchenko defends GLM-5.2 against claims of data contamination and Claude distillation
item.description: Public training traces reveal unsuccessful experiments with GRPO and OpenR1
item.content:encoded:
Public training traces reveal unsuccessful experiments with GRPO and OpenR1

Original source: https://posttrainbench.com/traces/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/evcaj0mx

Maksym Andriushchenko from the ELLIS Institute has pushed back on accusations that GLM-5.2's benchmark results reflect data contamination or direct Claude distillation by highlighting publicly available post-training traces that display varied reasoning across multiple seeds and a series of experiments that ultimately fell short. 1 27 3 7 1.4K FB MA #1210

  • Views: 1,518
  • Likes: 28
  • Bookmarks: 8
  • Replies: 1
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Maksym Andriushchenko @ ICML 🇰🇷
  • Florian Brand
@maksym_andr Why look at the data when you can make big claims instead

item.title: Meta's Lucas Beyer and builder Jason debate AI coding agent behavior after agent fixes tests before auditing
item.description: The debate focused on how autonomous agents sequence engineering tasks
item.content:encoded:
The debate focused on how autonomous agents sequence engineering tasks

Original source: https://x.com/giffmana/status/2073678159677915174

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/bhb0t505

A quick X exchange between Meta researcher Lucas Beyer and builder Jason spotlighted an AI coding agent that, after goal setup, decided to fix tests rather than move straight to an audit even though the task appeared wrapped up. 1 6 0 0 1.6K JA LB #72

  • Views: 1,665
  • Likes: 6
  • Replies: 1
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Lucas Beyer (bl16)
  • jason
@giffmana I meant the setup for the goals. 😿

item.title: KempeLab researchers analyze internalization, the process of training models to absorb chain-of-thought computations directly into parameters
item.description: The method succeeds on computationally difficult problems that resist direct learning.
item.content:encoded:
The method succeeds on computationally difficult problems that resist direct learning.

Original source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.20937

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/ir4kyc09

KempeLab researchers map out how transformers first master explicit chain-of-thought steps on tough tasks and then fold those steps straight into their weights, skipping token-by-token generation at inference time. 0 9 5 4 228 DF YD #322

  • Views: 18
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Yuntian Deng @ ICML
  • Dylan Foster 🐢
Check out our new paper on internalization: the process of gradually "absorbing" chain of thought computations during training. Our results show that internalization can work for problems that are computationally hard to learn directly. We carefully study method and task specific factors that determine internalization success. To learn more, see https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.20937. With @nikostsilivis @nirmitj_ @_rkomma Nati Srebro. @NYUDataScience @TTIC_Connect
